title: Total reclaimed space reports nonsensical value
body:
### Issue Description

After `podman system prune` podman reports that it reclaimed more than total disc capacity:
`Total reclaimed space: 198.4GB`

However, `df` reports "Avail" as 24G _after_ prune. Which is not possible by any means of compression given that data inside is mostly binary files or so.

In reality, podman reclaimed less than 10GB.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build and run stuff


### Describe the results you received

Nonsensical size of "reclaimed" space.

### Describe the results you expected

Real information of size that is reclaimed.

### podman info output

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I think the issue is in the sum logic of the reports. It doesn't distinguish between shared layers. So, if you run a build, each layer is counted independently, even if it’s actually shared between multiple images. Can you tell me what happened before `podman system prune`?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

> Can you tell me what happened before podman system prune?

I don't think anything spectacular. I just re-built a container lots of times in a process of making Dockerfile.
When it was done, I ran prune and size felt way off.

---

One thing to note though, lately I started to use `RUN --mount=type=cache` quite often. That might be useful too.

I have the same problem, here is some screenshot of the before and after. I also had the issue on my other computer. Before: After: Here is the output of the `podman version` command: I also made a small test after the prune from the screenshots, I built one of my service and this is the result: Before building: After building: After prune: Hope it helps!
@Meilky, can you provide a simplified Containerfile or the full one that you used to build the image?

@Honny1, here is the Containerfile content:

```
FROM docker.io/library/node:24

WORKDIR /usr/pack_man_frontend/

COPY ./package.json ./package-lock.json .

RUN npm install

COPY ./tsconfig.json ./tsconfig.app.json ./tsconfig.node.json ./svelte.config.js ./vite.config.ts .
COPY ./index.html .

COPY ./src ./src

RUN npm run check

RUN npm run build

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["npm", "run", "dev"]
```

I rebuild this container a lot. I assume since the code from `src` changes every build (since I use the container for development), it must create a lot of intermediate layer. In my small test, I only built it one time.
Also saw this, reclaimed 346GB on a 128 GB disk :)
